markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Normalize paths to repo/workspace-relative IDs and prevent hardcoded user profile or drive-letter authority. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: PathPortabilityNormalizer leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: PathPortabilityNormalizer is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 feab81f6; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: Homoglyph note (not a traversal escape): NFC normalization unifies composed/decomposed forms but does NOT collapse confusable scripts (e.g. Cyrillic 'а' vs Latin 'a'). Two visually identical filenames in different scripts produce DIFFERENT stored source paths -> two distinct source rows. This is a source-identity ambiguity, not a root-escape; acceptable for a path normalizer (collapsing scripts would corrupt legitimate non-Latin filenames).

normalize_source_relative_path delegates final-form validation to storage::knowledge::normalize_repo_relative_path AND the chk_*_path_portable DB CHECKs (defense in depth); the idempotence + storage-compat property test confirms outputs survive the storage normalizer unchanged.
evasion_probes: Drive letter (C:/, c:\, Z:relative) REJECTED CONFIRMED.
ADS file.txt:stream REJECTED via ':' check CONFIRMED.
Traversal ../, a/../b, trailing .. REJECTED (exact '..' segment match after '.'-collapse) CONFIRMED.
Control chars / NUL REJECTED via is_control() CONFIRMED.
Windows reserved device names CON/NUL/COM1..LPT9 (with or without extension via stem) REJECTED CONFIRMED; near-misses console.rs/nulled.txt stay valid.
Trailing dot/space segments REJECTED (Windows round-trip break) CONFIRMED.
Overlong-UTF-8 evasion: Rust str::from_utf8 (upstream in hashing.is_text) rejects overlong encodings before this normalizer sees text; non-UTF-8 paths cannot reach a String here.
Symlink escape: handled at the walk layer (engine.rs walk_files skips is_symlink) not here — cross-checked, no symlink target is ever normalized into a stored path.
evidence_checked: engine.rs:1305-1364 walk_files symlink skip + strip_prefix escape guard

evidence: Adversarial code review PASS/PASS_WITH_NOTES (ADVERSARIAL_CODE_REVIEWER-20260611-PM); only LOW non-blocking notes.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-083 requires a PathPortabilityNormalizer that canonicalizes ingested source paths to repo/workspace-relative POSIX IDs and rejects machine-local (drive-letter/rooted/UNC/ADS/traversal) authority, wired into the real ingestion pipeline with PostgreSQL/EventLedger proof. The code delivers exactly this: a real normalizer in paths.rs, genuinely wired into engine.rs ingestion call sites, with real Handshake-managed-PostgreSQL tests proving canonical storage and fail-closed rejection (zero durable rows on bad paths).
findings: paths.rs:45 normalize_source_relative_path is a real, complete implementation: backslash->'/' (line 61), UNC '//' reject (64), rooted '/' reject (69), drive-letter reject (75-81), ':' ADS/\\?\ catch (82-88), '..' traversal reject (96-100), '.'/empty-segment collapse (94), Windows reserved device names + trailing dot/space reject (102-112), NFC unification (123-127). Idempotent by construction.

Genuinely wired into the live ingestion path, not isolated: engine.rs:35 imports it and calls it at lines 248 (ingest_file_bytes), 282 (ingest_file_from_disk), and 594 (walk pass). At engine.rs:248-256 the normalized form is what flows into persist_attempt -> durable write, so the gate sits before durable records.

Real PostgreSQL proof in tests/knowledge_ingestion_policy_tests.rs mod mt_083_path_portability: machine_local_root_paths_are_rejected_before_any_durable_write (432) drives the real engine against the managed PG cluster, rejects C:/, C:\, /var, ../ and then asserts list_knowledge_source_roots and list_policy_decisions are BOTH empty (471-488) — true fail-closed, no mock. source_paths_normalize_to_repo_relative_posix_or_reject_typed (491) ingests 'deep\.\nested\note.md' and asserts stored relative_path == 'deep/nested/note.md' (523-527), then typed-rejects '..\escape.md','C:/abs.md','con.md','dir/file. '.

DB defense-in-depth is real: test at line 326-340 exercises the chk_knowledge_ingestion_policy_decisions_path_portable CHECK constraint at the DB boundary, and the idempotence test (paths.rs:222) confirms outputs survive storage::knowledge::normalize_repo_relative_path (knowledge.rs:230) unchanged.

Error type IngestionError::Validation(String) (mod.rs:84,96) is a real typed validation variant, not an *Unavailable / deferred-live placeholder; no todo!/unimplemented!/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-' on the live path.

Spec alignment: Master Spec v02.194 section 2.3.12 Storage Backend Portability Architecture (CX-DBP-001) makes repo-relative/portable paths canonical law; rejecting drive-letter/absolute machine-local authority matches it and [GLOBAL-PORTABILITY].

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): the V1 FAIL was purely an upstream-dependency timing flag (MT-081 not yet validator-complete), not a code defect; MT-081 is now resolved (commit 5876feaf) per remediation_v2. Packet cites v02.192 anchors vs current v02.194 — same section numbers resolve; not a fail reason.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
